evals.purpose: Confirm v3.2 doesn't break the existing standard-report flow, AND that the new `deep-research-agents-used:` envelope marker fires.
evals.prompt: Literature review on in-situ monitoring and evaluation methods for thermal and mechanical history in metal additive manufacturing. What sensor modalities and data types do researchers use to characterize the process, and how is this data used to detect defects or predict part quality? Use the deep-research skill in its standard `full` mode (don't request the new vault output).
evals.expected_output: Standard markdown report with numbered references AND a `deep-research-agents-used: [...]` line in the prologue. The agents-used list must include `paper_fetch_agent` and `bibliography_agent`.

evals.purpose: Exercise the new `synthesize` mode and confirm vault-shaped output (frontmatter + wikilinks + extracted_claims).
evals.prompt: Use the deep-research skill in `synthesize` mode. Topic: CCM (Convergent Cross Mapping) for detecting causality in noisy short-time-series — what's the consensus on its sensitivity to library length and embedding dimension, and where do recent papers (2023-2025) disagree? Output a single Synthesized Paper markdown page suitable for ingest into a personal Obsidian-flavored research wiki.
evals.expected_output: Single markdown file with YAML frontmatter (`type: synthesized-paper`, `topic`, `generated_by`, `deep-research-agents-used: [...]`, `sources: [[wikilinks]]`, `extracted_claims: [...]` with bidirectional shape `{id, quote, section, targets}`, `retrieval_quality_summary`), followed by Synthesis / Method-Family Map / Assumption Map / Sim-to-Real Summary / Gap Analysis / Sources sections. References as `[[paper-slug]]`, never `[1]/[2]`.

evals.purpose: Exercise the new `paper-review` mode and confirm vault-shaped Paper page with verbatim-quote-grounded extracted_claims.
evals.prompt: Use the deep-research skill in `paper-review` mode. Pick any recent (2023-2025) arxiv paper about causality detection methods (CCM, Granger causality, transfer entropy, or convergent cross-mapping variants) and produce a vault-shaped Paper page or `## Review` section. The paper must be one you actually fetch (not training-knowledge) and the extracted_claims[] must use verbatim quotes from the body with section refs.
evals.expected_output: Single markdown file with YAML frontmatter (`type: paper`, `authors`, `year`, `venue`, `url_or_path`, `review_status`, `generated_by`, `deep-research-agents-used: [...]`, `extracted_claims: [...]` with verbatim quotes, `retrieval_quality: full-text` or honest fallback), followed by `## Review` body and `## Connections suggested` section.
